title: Editas Medicine Pivots to In Vivo Gene Editing, Cuts Two-Thirds of Workforce
text: Editas Medicine announced significant restructuring on December 12, 2024, laying off approximately 180 employees, roughly two-thirds of its workforce, as part of strategic shift toward in vivo CRISPR therapies. The biotech company had previously announced in October it would seek commercial partner for reni-cel, its lead candidate for sickle cell disease. When partnership search failed to materialize, Editas decided to discontinue development of the therapy entirely. CEO Gilmore O'Neill stated that recent scientific breakthroughs have convinced them that timelines around near-term viability of in vivo CRISPR-edited medicines have accelerated meaningfully. This marks company's second major research reset in its 11-year history. Editas previously focused on inherited eye disorders before pivoting to blood disorders like sickle cell disease. Among those departing is Baisong Mei, company's chief medical officer, along with two board members resigning by year-end. Editas cited recent mouse studies demonstrating efficient delivery of CRISPR machinery directly into stem cells, eliminating need for external cell extraction and modification. Company plans to share additional preclinical data and development timelines in Q1 2025.
